page: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz, with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 128 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 397.95 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 191.37 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21464.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 46696.21, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core performance is average to strong, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1885.0. Cryptographic operations are supported via hardware AES acceleration, though other advanced vector and matrix extensions are absent.

The instance offers high resource density and memory throughput, making it highly cost-efficient for memory-bound and multi-threaded workloads without the premium of memory-optimized classes. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware limits its utility for high-speed local database caching or heavy parallel acceleration. It is highly suited for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, software compilation, static web serving, Redis deployments, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by 32 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 128 GB of RAM, a 15 Gbps baseline network speed, and no local storage or GPU hardware. The instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 397.95 GB/sec for large-block reads, alongside strong multi-core processing capabilities as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21464.0. While it lacks local NVMe storage and advanced matrix extensions, its high memory throughput and dedicated core allocation provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for balanced workloads. It is highly suited for database operations, software compilation, Redis caching, static web serving, and medium-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated ARM64 processor instance delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and balanced compute resources for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 ARM64 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 128 GB of system memory featuring top-tier read and write bandwidth
Delivers 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached resources
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and Redis caching
